feed.title: Gloamin - Blog
feed.subtitle: Gane is the day and mirk's the night | But we'll ne'er stray for faute o' light

entry.title: An Evening Walk
entry.content:
There is a particular moment at the edge of evening when the world seems to become quieter.

The sharp edges of the day begin to soften. The roads empty, the light fades, and familiar places take on a different character.

These are the hours I find myself returning to most often. Not quite day, not yet night. A small space between things.

Perhaps that is what draws me to making things on the web as well: the chance to create a small place apart from the noise.

entry.title: Notes on Making Things
entry.content:
I have always enjoyed the beginning of a project.

The first blank page. The empty directory. The moment before the shape of something has been decided.

There is a freedom in those early stages. Nothing is fixed yet, and every choice feels possible.

Of course, eventually the details arrive: structure, organisation, maintenance, and all the small decisions that turn an idea into something real.

But the beginning remains my favourite part.

A quiet room. A cup of tea. A few lines of code. Something slowly taking form.
